name: Common Trust
description_short: Member-owned credit union in Woburn, MA offering savings accounts, checking, loans, and credit cards with no closing costs on home equity lines and rates starting at prime minus 0.25%.
description_long:
Common Trust FCU is a federally-chartered credit union headquartered in Woburn, Massachusetts, serving the local community as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution. The organization operates under NCUA insurance and emphasizes its community-focused mission as a local credit union serving the greater Boston area.

Common Trust FCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including share savings accounts, share draft checking accounts, share certificates (CDs), vehicle loans, personal loans, real estate loans, and MasterCard credit cards. They provide specialized loan programs such as Mass Save HEAT loans for energy efficiency, credit builder loans for those with limited credit history (starting at $500 with rates as low as 9% APR), and tax season loans up to $5,000 with 12-month terms. The credit union also features online banking available 24/7, bill pay services, e-statements, and access to the CO-OP ATM network through CardNav.

Common Trust FCU distinguishes itself through competitive promotional offers including home equity lines of credit with no closing costs and rates as low as prime minus 0.25%. They provide a dedicated student membership package with tailored benefits and offer six $1,000 scholarships annually for graduating seniors. The institution maintains a Financial Education Center with free interactive courses covering money basics, loan management, homebuying, college planning, and retirement. They also partner with tax preparation services to offer member discounts during tax season.

As a credit union, Common Trust FCU operates on a member-owned, not-for-profit basis, meaning profits are returned to members through better rates and lower fees compared to traditional banks. However, as a smaller regional institution, their service footprint is limited primarily to the Woburn, Massachusetts area, and their online presence suggests limited information about specific membership eligibility criteria or detailed fee structures on their main website.
pros: Home equity lines of credit with NO closing costs and rates as low as prime minus 0.25%
Credit builder loans available for those with zero credit history at rates starting at 9% APR
Free 24/7 online banking with bill pay, e-statements, and account transfers included
Access to nationwide CO-OP ATM network through CardNav partnership
Free financial education center with interactive courses on homebuying, retirement, college planning, and fraud prevention
Specialized loan programs including Mass Save HEAT loans for energy efficiency improvements
Member scholarship program offering six $1,000 awards annually for graduating seniors
cons: Limited physical presence as a regional credit union based only in Woburn, MA
Website does not clearly display membership eligibility requirements or fee schedule details
Limited information provided about deposit rate competitiveness or CD terms on main website
No mention of mobile app features or specific online banking capabilities beyond basic services
Smaller institution may have fewer products and services compared to larger credit unions or national banks
best_for: Local Woburn, MA residents seeking community-focused banking with competitive rates
Individuals building credit for the first time or recovering from poor credit with credit builder loans
Homeowners looking to access home equity with no closing costs and favorable rates
Students seeking specialized membership benefits and scholarship opportunities
diagnosis: Common Trust FCU is best for residents of the Woburn, Massachusetts area seeking member-owned credit union banking with competitive rates on home equity and specialized programs for credit building. The main caveat is that as a regional institution, membership and service access are geographically limited, and prospective members should verify eligibility before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
